_metadata.file_description: Curated examples of local expert knowledge data sources (non-profit organizations, community groups, academic research, industry associations) for extreme weather risk assessment
_metadata.purpose: Provides starting point examples for agents. Agents must conduct external research beyond this file to find location-specific expert knowledge sources and current information.
_metadata.note: This file contains curated examples only. Agents should use these as starting points and conduct external research for current, location-specific expert knowledge sources. Building relationships with community organizations may be necessary for data access.

non_profit_organizations.description: Non-profit organizations providing environmental monitoring, community data, and local knowledge

sources.access_method: Public website, water quality reports, community resources, contact directly
sources.notes: Local non-profit providing water quality monitoring and environmental advocacy for Mobile Bay

sources.access_method: Public website, estuary program reports, contact directly
Public websites, environmental reports, contact directly
sources.notes: Various NGOs provide coastal environmental monitoring and advocacy

community_organizations.description: Community organizations providing local knowledge, traditional observations, and industry-specific data

sources.organization: Mobile Bay Fishing Union / Commercial Fishing Associations
sources.access_method: Contact directly, community meetings, industry reports
sources.notes: Commercial fishing organizations provide local knowledge and economic impact data

sources.access_method: Contact directly, community meetings, local organizations
sources.notes: Fishermen cooperatives provide local knowledge and traditional observations

sources.access_method: Contact directly, local government offices, community meetings
sources.notes: Village-level local government provides community-level data and traditional knowledge

academic_research.description: Academic institutions and research organizations providing scientific data and studies

sources.organization: University of South Alabama / Dauphin Island Sea Lab
sources.access_method: Research publications, data repositories, contact researchers
sources.notes: Academic institutions provide peer-reviewed research and scientific data

sources.access_method: Research publications, data repositories, contact researchers
Research publications, data repositories, contact researchers

industry_associations.description: Industry associations and trade organizations providing sector-specific data

sources.access_method: Public website, port reports, contact directly
Industry reports, association websites, contact directly
Public websites, port reports, contact directly
Chamber websites, industry reports, contact directly

indigenous_knowledge_holders.description: Indigenous knowledge holders and traditional knowledge keepers providing traditional observations and local knowledge

sources.organization: Indigenous Knowledge Holders / Traditional Knowledge Keepers
sources.access_method: Contact directly, relationship building, community engagement, respect cultural protocols
sources.notes: Indigenous knowledge holders provide traditional observations and cultural knowledge. Building respectful relationships and following cultural protocols is essential.

sources.access_method: Contact directly, relationship building, community engagement, respect cultural protocols
sources.notes: Indigenous communities provide traditional observations and cultural knowledge. Building respectful relationships and following cultural protocols is essential.

environmental.description: Environmental data including water quality, air quality, ecosystem health

local_knowledge.description: Local knowledge and traditional observations from community organizations and residents

economic.description: Economic data including development trends, investment data, industry information

scientific_research.description: Scientific research data and studies from academic institutions

access_methods.public_websites: Some organizations provide data through public websites and reports
access_methods.direct_contact: Many community organizations require direct contact and relationship building
access_methods.community_engagement: Community organizations may require participation in community meetings or events
access_methods.research_repositories: Academic institutions often provide data through research repositories
access_methods.relationship_building: For community and indigenous knowledge sources, building respectful relationships is essential
access_methods.cultural_protocols: When accessing indigenous or traditional knowledge, following cultural protocols is required
access_methods.note: Access methods vary significantly by organization type. Community and indigenous knowledge sources often require relationship building and respect for cultural protocols.

best_practices.verify_sources: Always verify that organizations exist and are accessible before referencing them
best_practices.check_data_freshness: Verify data freshness and update frequency for each source
best_practices.respect_data_ownership: Respect data ownership, licensing requirements, and cultural protocols
best_practices.build_relationships: For community organizations and indigenous knowledge holders, building respectful relationships is essential
best_practices.follow_cultural_protocols: When accessing indigenous or traditional knowledge, follow cultural protocols and respect community guidelines
best_practices.cite_sources: Always cite expert knowledge sources appropriately and with permission when required
best_practices.acknowledge_contributions: Acknowledge contributions from community organizations and knowledge holders
best_practices.note: Agents should research current access methods and data availability for each location and organization type. Building relationships with community organizations may be necessary for data access.
